svg.title: A strategy game's resource-to-unit pipeline: Gold, Food, and Wood feed a Barracks that trains Soldiers and Archers
svg.desc: A three-column horizontal pipeline showing how an RTS resource economy feeds military production. A header band at the top labels the three columns RESOURCES, PRODUCTION, and UNITS. The left column has three stacked resource sources. Mine shows a mountain icon with a gold nugget and the stat Gold plus ten per second. Farm shows three wheat stalks with grain heads and the stat Food plus eight per second. Forest shows a small tree with a green canopy and brown trunk and the stat Wood gathered by worker. The center column has one large cell labeled BARRACKS with a shield-and-crossed-swords icon, a build cost pill reading three hundred gold plus one hundred wood, and a list of four units it can train: Soldier costing one hundred gold plus twenty food, Archer costing one hundred twenty gold plus twenty food, Cavalry costing two hundred gold plus fifty food, and Siege costing three hundred gold plus one hundred wood. The right column has two stacked unit cells. Soldier shows a vertical sword icon, stats of one hundred hit points and fifteen attack, and a cost pill of one hundred gold plus twenty food. Archer shows a curved bow with a horizontal arrow icon, stats of sixty hit points, twelve attack, and range five, and a cost pill of one hundred twenty gold plus twenty food. Three dashed colored curves funnel from each resource cell into the Barracks left edge: amber from Mine, green from Farm, and brown from Forest. Two dashed blue arrows fan from the Barracks right edge to the Soldier and Archer cells.

g.text: Each unit consumes resources from above
and joins your army on the right
